nativeRange.habitat: Shallow warm coastal waters, tide pools, reef flats, and lagoons. Found in all oceans except the Arctic.

avoidWith: aggressive tang and herbivore fish if display use — tangs and other herbivores will consume the algae

summary: A popular genus-level Gracilaria entry representing numerous trade forms (thick, branching, and ornamental red macroalgae) widely used for nutrient export in refugiums and as tang food.
careNotes:
Thick Ogo is a genus-level trade entry (Gracilaria sp.) consolidating the AlgaeBarn 'Thick Ogo' listing alongside ~18 additional Gracilaria sp. trade forms from Top Shelf Aquatics and Mosaic Macros (Bird's Nest, Purple Whip, Red Bamboo, Orange Thornbush, Pink Fan, Corky Whip, Succulent Orange, Cream Knobby, Crimson Tumbleweed, Corky Bamboo, Green Ivy, Pink Serpent Tongue, and others — see alsoKnownAs). No species epithet is applied per genus-level trade convention; see [red-ogo] (G. parvispora), [pom-pom] (G. hayi), [green-ogo-gracilaria-tikvahiae] (G. tikvahiae), [red-spaghetti] (G. pacifica), and [monkey-finger] (G. salicornia) for named Gracilaria siblings with species-level identification.

Gracilaria is one of the most widely cultivated macroalgae genera in the hobby. It tends to grow rapidly in well-lit, high-flow refugiums and is generally considered among the best nutrient export macroalgae for reef tanks. Clippings can be fed directly to tangs, surgeonfish, and other marine herbivores as a highly palatable food source.

The genus is distributed across all major tropical and subtropical oceans (absent from Arctic waters). It generally prefers water temperatures above 22°C and may decline at lower temperatures. Water parameters should be maintained in typical reef ranges: temperature 22–28°C, salinity SG 1.023–1.025, pH 7.8–8.4, alkalinity dKH 8–12. Trace supplementation with iron and iodine has been reported to support growth when natural aquarium nutrients are insufficient, though this is rarely necessary in established reef systems.

Refugium placement is most common — tumbling freely in high flow under a grow light. Can also be placed in display tanks as an ornamental red macro, but tangs and other herbivores will consume it readily. Propagation is by simple fragmentation; trimming to harvest for tang feeding also naturally controls spread. No sexual propagation is typically attempted in captivity.
breedingNotes: Propagated in captivity exclusively by thallus fragmentation. Trim and replant cuttings. No aquarium-level gametophyte/sporophyte alternation propagation documented.

additional.name: Top Shelf Aquatics — Red Ogo Gracilaria
additional.notes: Secondary cross-check. Page confirms refugium + flow use and nutrient export but lacks detailed water parameter specs. Water parameters from AlgaeBarn primary.

additional.name: Top Shelf Aquatics — Pom Pom Gracilaria
additional.notes: Additional TSA Gracilaria sp. listing — confirms refugium tumbling, ample flow, nutrient export (nitrates, phosphates, DOC), and compatibility note that tangs/herbivores will consume it.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros — Bird's Nest Gracilaria
additional.notes: Mosaic Macros Gracilaria sp. listing. Confirms red branching morphology. Care parameters not stated; image candidate sourced.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Gracilaria
additional.notes: Taxonomy confirmed: Family Gracilariaceae, Order Gracilariales, Class Florideophyceae, Division Rhodophyta. Distribution: all oceans except Arctic; cannot survive below 10°C. AlgaeBase blocked by CAPTCHA verification — Wikipedia used as taxonomy fallback.
